sentence: قُلْتُ لَك الْيَوم رَاح أَزُورَك بِالْبَيت.
raw: قلت لك اليوم راح أزورك بالبيت.
ipa: ˈqultu ˈlak alˈjoːm ˈraːħ ʔaˈzuːrak bilˈbeːt
gloss_en: I told you that today I will visit you at home.
notes: qeltu-diagnostic qultu 'I said' with /q/ (blanc1964 §1: the qeltu/gilit split); future rāḥ

gloss_en: The coffee in Mosul is good and the people are generous.
notes: ق=/q/ retained in qahwa (blanc1964; jasim2020: Mosul qeltu); geminate يّ; sun assimilation an-n

sentence: مَا أَعْرِف ثَلَاثَة أَشْخَاص يِحْكُون مِثْلَك.
raw: ما أعرف ثلاثة أشخاص يحكون مثلك.
ipa: ˈmaː ˈʔaʕʁif θaˈlaːθa ʔaʃˈxɑːsˤ jiħˈkuːn ˈmiθlak
gloss_en: I don't know three people who talk like you.
notes: interdental ث retained /θ/ (blanc1964: qeltu retains interdentals); yiḥkūn 'they speak' with k (NO affrication, unlike gilit)

sentence: هَاذَا الظَّابُط قَال كِلْشِي رَاح يَكُون زَين.
raw: هاذا الظابط قال كلشي راح يكون زين.
ipa: ˈhaːðaː ðˤˈðˤɑːbutˤ ˈqaːl ˈkilʃiː ˈraːħ jaˈkuːn ˈzeːn
gloss_en: This officer said everything will be fine.
notes: ظ=/ðˤ/ retained; ق=/q/ in qāl (vs gilit gāl, blanc1964 §3.25); kilši 'everything' (jasim2020)

sentence: شْقَد صَار عَدَد التَّلَامِيذ بِصَفّ الْعَرَبِي؟
raw: شقد صار عدد التلاميذ بصف العربي؟
ipa: ˈʃqad ˈsˤɑːr ˈʕadad attalaːˈmiːð biˈsˤɑff alˈʕarabiː
gloss_en: How many pupils are there now in the Arabic class?
notes: šqad 'how much' with /q/ (jasim2020); ذ retained /ð/ in talāmīð; sun assimilation at-t

gloss_en: Get up, open the door and bring the tea.
notes: qeltu imperatives qum/iftah/jib keep /q/ and the affricate ǧīm (blanc1964) — the gilit contrast in one frame

sentence: الْيَوم الْجَوّ بَارِد وَمَا أَكُو شَمِس.
raw: اليوم الجو بارد وما أكو شمس.
ipa: alˈjoːm alˈdʒaww ˈbaːrid ˈwamaː ˈʔakuː ˈʃamis
gloss_en: Today the weather is cold and there is no sun.
notes: aku/maku 'there is/isn't' pan-Iraqi existential; ay->eː monophthong in yōm (jasim2020: OA /aj/>[eː] in qeltu); geminate جّ ww kept; negation mā

sentence: بِكَم كِيلُو الطَّمَاطَة؟ بِأَلْف وْخَمِس مِيَة.
raw: بكم كيلو الطماطة؟ بألف وخمس مية.
ipa: ˈbikam ˈkiːluː tˤtˤɑˈmɑːtˤɑ biˈʔalf ˈwxamis ˈmija
gloss_en: How much is a kilo of tomatoes? One thousand five hundred.
notes: plain /k/ in kīlū — no affrication (jasim2020 §2.9.2, vs gilit); sun assimilation aṭ-ṭ; number alf w-xamsimya

gloss_en: Our appointment is at seven o'clock by the mosque.
notes: ǧīm [dʒ] retained in jāmiʕ (blanc1964; jasim2020 §2.4); aw->oː in mawʕid; number sabʕa

gloss_en: My father lives in Mosul with our family.
notes: plain /k/ in yiskun (jasim2020 §2.9.2 — no kaf affrication in Muslawi qeltu); Mosul the qeltu heartland (blanc1964 §2); wiyya 'with' Iraqi

sentence: رُوح دُغْرِي وْلِفّ عَ الْيَمِين عِنْد الْجَامِع.
raw: روح دغري ولف ع اليمين عند الجامع.
ipa: ˈruːħ ˈduɣʁiː ˈwliff ˈʕa ljaˈmiːn ˈʕind alˈdʒaːmiʕ
gloss_en: Go straight, then turn right by the mosque.
notes: ǧīm [dʒ] in jāmiʕ (blanc1964); geminate ff kept; imperatives rūḥ/liff; ʕa+article written as two tokens

gloss_en: The food was good and the broth was fine.
notes: plain /k/ in kān — the qeltu reflex vs gilit čān (jasim2020 §2.9.2, thesis minimal pair p.23); /q/ retained in maraq (blanc1964 §3.26); ay->eː zēn

gloss_en: Where did my sister go? I haven't seen her.
notes: lwēn 'to where' ay->eː monophthong (jasim2020: OA /aj/>[eː]); negation mā; ʔuxt attested qeltu (jasim2020-024)

gloss_en: How many pens do you have in the bag?
notes: the three qeltu diagnostics in one frame: /q/ in qalam (blanc1964 §3.26), plain /k/ in kam (jasim2020 §2.9.2), ǧīm [dʒ] in janṭa; number kam

gloss_en: If you come tomorrow, we will talk together.
notes: 'tomorrow' bākir with plain /k/ — qeltu vs gilit bāčir (jasim2020 §2.9.2); ǧīm [dʒ] in tiji; interdental ذ /ð/ retained in iḏā (blanc1964 §3.2)

gloss_en: Yesterday I went to the market and brought bread.
notes: /q/ retained in sūq (blanc1964 §3.26); ǧīm [dʒ] in jibit 'I brought'; xubz attested Mesopotamian (jasim2020-009)

gloss_en: Tomorrow we will travel to Baghdad by train.
notes: /q/ retained in qiṭār (blanc1964 §3.26); plain /k/ in bākir (jasim2020 §2.9.2); future rāḥ

sentence: أَلُو، مِنُو يِحْكِي؟ رَاح أَتَّصِل بِيك بَاكِر.
raw: ألو، منو يحكي؟ راح أتصل بيك باكر.
ipa: ˈʔaluː ˈminuː ˈjiħkiː ˈraːħ ˈʔattɑsˤil ˈbiːk ˈbaːkiʁ
gloss_en: Hello, who is speaking? I will call you tomorrow.
notes: plain /k/ in yiḥki, bīk, bākir — no affrication (jasim2020 §2.9.2); minu 'who' Iraqi; geminate ttaṣil

gloss_en: How are you? Fine, praise God.
notes: šlōnak 'how are you' with plain /k/ and aw->oː (jasim2020); greeting adjacency pair; ay->eː zēn

gloss_en: Patience is the key to relief.
notes: ǧīm [dʒ] retained in faraj (blanc1964; jasim2020 §2.4); sun assimilation aṣ-ṣ; closing proverb
